short_description: Colorized circular cover featuring an ermine and mountain scenery.
description: A circular cast-iron sewer cover painted with green, light blue, and white accents. The main design depicts a stylized, cartoon-like ermine (stoat) in the foreground, set against a background of rugged mountain peaks and rolling clouds. Text at the top reads '高山市' (Takayama City) in a light blue banner, and text to the right reads '奥飛騨温泉郷' (Okuhida Onsengo). A light blue ribbon under the animal reads '下水道' (Sewerage), and the central circular emblem displays the former Kamitara village crest.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-chubu-sangaku-national-park--animal--01.jpg'. This cover is located in the Okuhida Hot Spring Village area near Chubu-Sangaku National Park, celebrating the region's alpine wildlife.

short_description: Unpainted variant of the Okuhida Onsengo ermine cover.
description: An unpainted, raw cast-iron circular cover featuring the same alpine design as the colorized version. It illustrates a stylized ermine with mountains and clouds in low-relief texture. Instead of kanji banners at the top right, this casting variant features a central ribbon reading 'OKUHIDA' in English block lettering next to the central crest, a small top arc reading '平湯' (Hirayu), and '下水道' (Sewerage) along the bottom banner.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-chubu-sangaku-national-park--animal--02.jpg'. A textured, high-traction variant of the Okuhida design series found near the Hirayu Onsen area.

short_description: Rectangular drainage grate inset showing two ducks in flight.
description: A decorative, rectangular cast-iron panel set into a standard urban storm drainage grate. The colored illustration features two ducks (mallards) flying closely together over stylized water waves, with a vibrant patch of blue sky visible in the upper right background. One duck clearly showcases a green painted head characteristic of a male mallard.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-hakone--ducks--01.jpg'. Located near Lake Ashi, celebrating the local waterfowl ecosystem.

short_description: Rectangular storm drain inset featuring a large fish swimming.
description: A rectangular artistic utility plate integrated into a roadside drainage grate system. The center panel depicts a large fish—resembling a largemouth or black bass—swimming through clear water. The water is highlighted with turquoise-blue paint, contrasted against textured dark iron waves and a tan-painted fish body.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-hakone--fish--01.jpg'. This public design references Lake Ashi's popularity as a famous freshwater sport fishing destination.

short_description: Blue-painted circular valve cover featuring a lily and geometric waves.
description: A circular cast-iron utility gate valve cover heavily coated in bright blue paint. The detailed layout features a large blooming Hakone lily (Shirayuri) on the right side, flanked by dynamic, geometric water waves on the bottom. The left side includes an abstract municipal or prefectural alignment motif. Bold kanji characters at the bottom center read '仕切弁' (Sikiriben - Gate Valve/Shut-off Valve). The outer ring is inscribed with repeating segments reading '県水道' (Ken Suido - Prefectural Waterworks).
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-hakone--flower--01.jpg'. Managed by the Kanagawa Prefectural Waterworks Bureau, featuring the symbolic town flora of Hakone.

short_description: Rectangular drainage panel depicting a pair of swans on a lake.
description: A decorative rectangular cast-iron storm drain inset panel. The color-filled relief graphic displays a pair of elegant tan-colored swans swimming side-by-side on smooth water. The background and water reflections are filled with a rich sky-blue paint layer, framed by a dark iron border and a square-tread traction grid.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-hakone--swans--01.jpg'. Part of the lakeside series highlighting migratory birds and native wildlife found around Hakone's waterways.

short_description: Rectangular fire hydrant with flying heron and orchids.
description: A rectangular, high-visibility cast-iron fire hydrant cover set flush into textured sidewalk tiles. The inner plate is colorfully detailed with a soft blue sky and green background, showcasing a majestic white heron or egret (Shirasagi)—the official bird of Himeji—flying above a cluster of blooming White Egret Orchids (Sagiso). A stylized silhouette of Himeji Castle's roofline is nestled in the center. The bottom left marker reads '消火栓' (Fire Hydrant) and the bottom right reads 'ひめじ' (Himeji).
notes: This design honors Himeji's identity as the 'White Heron City'.

short_description: Cream-colored cover showing grand Himeji Castle keep.
description: A beautifully rendered circular sewer cover centered around a detailed graphic of Himeji Castle (White Heron Castle) against a cream-colored sky with stylized clouds. The gray and white lines accentuate the stone foundations and multi-tiered rooftops of the UNESCO World Heritage site. Bold black kanji script on the left reads '世界遺産 姫路城' (World Heritage Himeji Castle). The bottom edge features a stone wall texture texture and a small white block reading 'OSUI' (Wastewater).
notes: The quintessential tourist design cover celebrating Japan's most iconic surviving samurai castle.

short_description: Rectangular diamond tread valve cover with city crest.
description: A heavy-duty rectangular industrial utility valve cover with an anti-slip diamond cross-hatch tread pattern across its surface. The center features a smooth circular plate enclosing the classic Himeji municipal crest. Arranged symmetrically around the outer edges of the central crest are four individual kanji characters: '水' (Water), '道' (Way/Road), '消' (Extinguish), and '火' (Fire), identifying its place within the municipal water system.
notes: A durable, functional infrastructure asset optimized for urban sidewalk pathways.

short_description: Blue and cream cover depicting herons over reeds.
description: A vibrant circular wastewater cover bordered by a light teal rim. The main illustration features a flock of seven stylized white herons (Shirasagi) in mid-flight against a background of undulating blue and white water waves. Below the birds is a detailed network of green wetlands and wild grasses mimicking the shape of White Egret Orchids. The bottom border contains blue text reading 'ひめじ' (Himeji) and 'おすい' (Osui - Wastewater).
notes: An artistic public installation that heavily reinforces the natural ecosystem symbols of the city.

short_description: Commemorative Gundam cover standing beside Himeji Castle.
description: A beautifully detailed, colorized circular cover featuring the legendary RX-78-2 Gundam standing prominently in defense beside the grand multi-tiered main keep of Himeji Castle (White Heron Castle). The background consists of geometric sky shards in shades of purple and blue. A salmon pink text block at the bottom reads '姫路城' (Himeji Castle). The off-white outer border is boldly inscribed with 'HIMEJI', 'RX-78-2 GUNDAM', and '©SOTSU・SUNRISE'.
notes: Part of the highly celebrated nationwide 'Gundam Manhole Project' initiative to blend pop culture with historic local heritage.

short_description: Standard unpainted wastewater cover with honeycomb traction grid.
description: An unpainted, circular cast-iron wastewater cover featuring a dense, skid-resistant honeycomb pattern across its surface. Small raised circular tokens are distributed evenly around the midsection of the grid. The center contains a circular emblem with the Himeji city crest—a stylized representation of a white heron pattern. A small curved banner at the bottom contains the text 'おすい' (Osui - Wastewater).
notes: A utility-focused street asset commonly found throughout urban areas of Himeji.

short_description: Colorized Gundam cover featuring Zaku II and museum.
description: A striking circular commemorative cover painted in olive green, red-brown, salmon pink, and cream tones. The design depicts the iconic MS-06F Zaku II mobile suit wielding a Heat Hawk axe. In the foreground is a detailed rendering of the historic red-brick Himeji City Museum of Art, with the silhouette of Himeji Castle visible in the background. A prominent yellow title block reads '姫路市立美術館' (Himeji City Museum of Art). The outer lime green rim is labeled 'HIMEJI', 'MS-06F Zaku II', and '©SOTSU・SUNRISE'.
notes: Installed as part of the official 'Gundam Manhole Project' to boost local tourism and celebrate the city's landmarks.

short_description: Unpainted cast-iron cover with a radial geometric pattern.
description: An unpainted, circular cast-iron utility cover featuring a clean, geometric layout. The design is structured around concentric circles intersected by radial lines, creating a grid of panels. Within the outer rings, each section houses a stylized circular motif with a central dot, while the exact center of the cover remains a smooth, unadorned circular plate.
notes: Photographed on a gravel walkway surface, showcasing a utility design focused entirely on geometric symmetry and traction.

short_description: Rectangular fire hydrant lid with a textured honeycomb grid.
description: A high-visibility rectangular utility cover set flush into sidewalk tiling. The central cast-iron plate features a dense, skid-resistant honeycomb traction pattern. Raised kanji on the plate reads '上水道' (Waterworks) at the top, '消火栓' (Fire Hydrant) at the bottom, and a large central circle enclosing the character '水' (Water). The surrounding frame is painted tan with bold red corner blocks containing the warning text '駐', '車', '禁', '止' (No Parking).
notes: A standard municipal emergency infrastructure asset optimizing street visibility and safety compliance.

short_description: Colorized cover depicting Edo-period pilgrims on a journey.
description: A beautifully rendered circular sewer cover illustrating 'Okage-mairi'—the historic mass pilgrimages to the Grand Shrine of Ise during the Edo period. The painted design shows five travelers in traditional clothing, straw hats, and cloaks journeying together under a soft teal sky with stylized clouds. Large bold kanji at the top reads '伊勢' (Ise). A green banner at the bottom contains the hiragana text 'おすい' (Osui - Wastewater).
notes: This signature design beautifully honors Ise's deep cultural history as Japan's ultimate pilgrimage destination.

short_description: Unpainted relief variant of the Ise pilgrim scene.
description: An unpainted, raw cast-iron circular wastewater cover utilizing the exact same 'Okage-mairi' pilgrimage illustration as the colorized version. The relief details the expressions, woven textures of the straw hats, and walking staffs of the five historic travelers. The large characters for '伊勢' (Ise) remain prominent at the top, and the text 'おすい' (Osui - Wastewater) is embedded along the bottom rim.
notes: The standard, unpainted street-level deployment of Ise's iconic historical narrative layout.

short_description: Colorized orange and gray circular fire hydrant cover.
description: A circular cast-iron fire hydrant cover inset into an orange-bordered street frame. The outer ring of the cover is highlighted with a vivid orange paint layer featuring repeating wave or cloud abstract cuts alongside the vertical text '県営水道' (Prefectural Waterworks). The center area displays a dense field of flying gulls over ripples, with a central crest containing the Kamakura City gentian flower (Sashindo) emblem and a bottom orange text block reading '消火栓' (Fire Hydrant).
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kamakura--fire--01.jpg'. Managed under Kanagawa Prefectural Waterworks, utilizing Kamakura's local municipal symbols.

short_description: Unpainted circular valve cover featuring a lily flower design.
description: An unpainted, circular cast-iron gate valve cover. The relief artwork is split between a stylized lily blossom (Shirayuri) on the right and geometric wave layers on the bottom, with an abstract municipal wave alignment pattern dominating the upper-left quadrant. Text in low relief reads '仕切弁' (Gate Valve) next to the Kanagawa water bureau symbol, surrounded by '県水道' (Prefectural Waterworks) repeating on the outer ring.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kamakura--flower--01.jpg'. This standard regional Kanagawa Prefecture asset layout is also seen across nearby municipal boundaries like Hakone.

short_description: Unpainted diamond grid cover featuring Kamakura's gentian crest.
description: A classic circular cast-iron utility cover with a raised diamond-shaped cross-hatch traction grid across its entire surface. At the geometric center, a smooth circular medallion contains the historical Kamakura City crest—the gentian flower and leaves (Sashindo). The cover includes two small teardrop utility lifting slots on opposite sides.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kamakura--industrial--01.jpg'. A functional heavy-traffic urban infrastructure cover utilizing Kamakura's official city emblem for local identification.

short_description: Clean-cast diamond grid variant featuring the city gentian crest.
description: A circular cast-iron cover featuring a sharp diamond grid pattern designed for pedestrian skid resistance. The center is anchored by the Kamakura City gentian flower symbol enclosed in a clean circle. The composition is highly similar to industrial--01 but displays slight casting finish differences and alternate alignment of its perimeter features.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kamakura--industrial--02.jpg'. Another standard street variant showing the deep deployment of the municipal emblem on basic utility hardware.

short_description: Unpainted artistic cover with cherry blossoms and checkerboard patterns.
description: An unpainted circular cast-iron utility cover featuring a rich, complex mix of traditional patterns. Diagonal bands of flowing, stylized water waves intersect with blocks of 'Ichimatsu' (traditional Japanese checkerboard patterns). Sprigs of blooming cherry blossoms (Sakura) are delicately scattered throughout the composition. A small circular municipal crest sits directly in the center.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kamakura--industrial--03.jpg'. An unpainted deployment of a high-art design that weaves classic Japanese textile and natural motifs into the urban landscape.

short_description: Radial geometric cover for a fire defense water cistern.
description: A circular cast-iron utility cover with a prominent concentric and radial geometric block pattern. The inner ring features raised kanji characters reading '防火' (Fire Defense/Prevention) at the top and '貯水槽' (Water Cistern / Storage Tank) at the bottom. The center circle prominently displays the Kamakura City gentian flower crest.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kamakura--industrial--04.jpg'. This designates an underground emergency water storage supply intended for local firefighting use.

short_description: Vibrant teal-blue sewer cover depicting traditional mizuhiki paper-cord braiding.
description: A circular cast-iron wastewater cover with an inset decorative plate. The centerpiece is a brilliant teal-blue disc filled with a complex, intersecting circular geometric relief layout that mimics traditional 'Mizuhiki' (decorative Japanese paper-cord) knot-tying and braiding techniques. The raw dark-iron outer rim contains segmented anti-slip rib patterns, the city's stylized text on the bottom left reading 'かなざわし' (Kanazawa City), and 'げすい' (Osui - Wastewater) on the bottom right.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kanazawa--blue--01.jpg'. This public design pays homage to Kanazawa's esteemed history as a UNESCO City of Crafts and Folk Arts.

short_description: Rectangular cast-iron drainage grate embedded with the Kanazawa city crest.
description: A heavy-duty rectangular storm water catch basin grate set into a stone block sidewalk pathway. The grate features vertical drainage slots intersected by crossbars, bookended on the top and bottom by traction zones patterned with small raised pebbles. Perfectly dead-centered within the slots is a cast circular medallion housing the official Kanazawa City crest—the plum blossom outline surrounding the stylized kanji character for '金' (Kana).
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kanazawa--drain--01.jpg'. Shows how municipal branding is elegantly down-scaled onto standard roadside stormwater infrastructure.

short_description: Green-rimmed circular gas valve cover with a honeycomb texture.
description: A small circular cast-iron utility cap inset into a concrete stone pavement tile. The outer ring is coated in a durable, bright green textured paint with repeating traction dimples. The interior bare-iron surface utilizes a dense, defensive honeycomb mesh pattern flanking a raised vertical rectangular title box that reads 'ガスバルブ' (Gas Valve). To the left of the text box is a circular stamp enclosing the city's plum blossom crest wrapped around a stylized 'G' for gas.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kanazawa--green--01.jpg'. High-contrast industrial design utilizing color coding for gas infrastructure line maintenance safety.

short_description: Unpainted heavy utility cover with a geometric fretwork pattern.
description: An unpainted, circular cast-iron electrical access hatch set flush into an aggregate asphalt street. The cover features a highly dense, slip-resistant continuous key fretwork or maze-like structural grid profile. The center features a circular stamp enclosing Kanazawa's historic plum blossom and '金' (Kana) crest, positioned right above a raised horizontal rectangular label reading '電気' (Electricity). A tiny manufacturer diamond stamp at the very bottom edge reads 'FUKUNISHI'.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kanazawa--industrial--01.jpg'. A durable, functional street fixture utilizing standard anti-slip safety patterns common to electrical lines.

short_description: Unpainted circular concentric ring utility cover.
description: A standard circular cast-iron utility cover with a plain, unpainted finish. The layout consists of blocky, rectangular traction teeth arranged in four nesting concentric rings that radiate outwards from the center. The absolute core of the cover features a circular crest enclosing the classic Kanazawa plum flower emblem. A single utility lifting notch breaks the alignment at the bottom rim.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kanazawa--industrial--02.jpg'. A generic municipal design utilized across public spaces to balance traction efficiency and low-maintenance functionality.

short_description: Colorized rectangular fire hydrant lid showing the Kotoji stone lantern.
description: A rectangular painted cast-iron emergency fire utility cover. The artwork beautifully illustrates the famous two-legged Kotoji-toro (stone lantern) of Kenroku-en Garden, widely treated as the primary visual symbol of Kanazawa. The golden-tan lantern stands over swirling teal-blue streams of water, framed against a rich forest-green background scattered with floating autumn maple leaves. Bold raised kanji at the bottom right reads '消火栓' (Fire Hydrant).
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kanazawa--lantern--01.jpg'. One of Kanazawa's most universally recognized and celebrated local landmark configurations.

short_description: Official PokéFuta cover featuring the Pokémon Milotic.
description: An official colorized PokéFuta (Pokémon cover) situated outside the National Crafts Museum. The central design shows the elegant water-type Pokémon Milotic arcing gracefully over a stylized circle decorated with Kutani-yaki porcelain patterns, traditional crafts, and a central Mizuhiki string knot. The artwork is bordered by a sky-blue rim stating 'ミロカロス' (Milotic) along the top, and 'かなざわ' (Kanazawa) alongside '©Pokémon' at the bottom.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kanazawa--pokeman--01.jpg'. Unveiled in May 2023, Milotic was selected to mirror Kanazawa's status as an epicenter of natural beauty, fine art, and craft culture.

short_description: Square bronze commemorative highway marker plaque for Route 159.
description: A decorative square metal marker plaque installed flush among sidewalk paving stones. The top section showcases low-relief cast artwork depicting delicate cherry blossom flowers floating on stylized flowing river currents. The bottom half is split into two panels: the left displays a pink-shaded shield crest bearing the number '159' (signifying National Route 159), while the right side displays the large bronze number '65'.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kanazawa--street-plaque--01.jpg'. An aesthetic urban boundary, distance tracking, or infrastructure management plaque highlighting local natural motifs.

short_description: Mount Fuji and the Kawaguchiko Ohashi Bridge.
description: This cover captures a classic postcard view from the northern shore of Lake Kawaguchi. Mt. Fuji dominates the center-right of the design with its snow-capped silhouette. Kawaguchiko Ohashi Bridge (河口湖大橋), the concrete structure crossing the left side of the cover, spans Lake Kawaguchi and offers panoramic views of Mt. Fuji to travelers crossing the lake.
notes: May be from Nagashima Foundry

short_description: Legacy Ashiwada Village cover featuring lilies and pines.
description: An unpainted, industrial circular iron cover featuring an abstract, geometric layout. The outer rim is decorated with a repeating pattern of stylized crowns or cloud-like crests representing pine needles. The inner circle contains dynamic, mirror-imaged branches of Suzuran (Lily of the Valley) flowers surrounding a central mountain hexagram mark. A rectangular label at the lower center reads 'おすい' (Osui - Wastewater).
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kawaguchiko--crowns--01.jpg'. This vintage design highlights the historic symbols of the former Ashiwada Village.

short_description: Colorized fire cistern cover depicting a traditional matoi.
description: A striking circular fire protection cover painted in bold yellow, soft purple, and dark grey tones. The upper half features a geometric diamond cube housing a stylized snowflake-like crest. Spilling downward from the cube is a massive, stylized rendering of a 'matoi'—the traditional tasseled standard used by Edo-period fire brigades to signal fire locations. Bold kanji text splits across the center reading '防火' (Boka - Fire Prevention) and '水槽' (Suiso - Water Tank/Cistern). The old Kawaguchiko town mark is enclosed in a badge on the right.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kawaguchiko--fire--01.jpg'. A fantastic example of emergency infrastructure combining high visibility with historic local fire-fighting iconography.

short_description: Unpainted Fujikawaguchiko cover showcasing regional town flora.
description: An unpainted, circular cast-iron sewer cover featuring a dense floral arrangement of Fujikawaguchiko's regional plants. The design highlights blooming Fuji Cherry (Fujizakura) blossoms along with Evening Primrose (Tsukimiso) flowers, set against an abundance of textured leaves. At the center is a circular emblem depicting the stylized municipal crest of the former Kawaguchiko Town. A small banner at the bottom contains the text 'おすい' (Osui - Wastewater).
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kawaguchiko--leaves--01.jpg'. This design elegantly preserves the legacy flora symbols of the area prior to regional mergers.

short_description: Alternative flora cover variation featuring central hexagram emblem.
description: An unpainted circular cast-iron utility cover bearing a similar floral pattern of Fuji Cherry and Evening Primrose blooms to the standard town design. However, the exact center features a geometric hexagram (six-pointed star) emblem enclosing a stylized mountain pinnacle. The bottom text bar reads 'おすい' (Osui - Wastewater). The entire cover is framed by a distinct triangular spike border pattern along the outer edge.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kawaguchiko--leaves-and-star--01.jpg'. An intriguing localized variation within the lakeside utility network.

short_description: Historical Katsuyama Village cover depicting playful squirrels.
description: A beautifully detailed, unpainted circular cover showcasing two playful squirrels surrounded by oak branches and acorns. One squirrel is perched on a branch eating an acorn, while the second stands below it looking upward. The entire central scene is framed by an elegant border of blooming cherry blossoms. The bottom left edge explicitly denotes the historical locality text 'かつやま' (Katsuyama), right next to 'おすい' (Osui - Wastewater).
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kawaguchiko--squirrels--01.jpg'. A highly sought-after legacy design from the former Katsuyama Village area before it merged into modern Fujikawaguchiko.

short_description: Rectangular drainage grate with maple leaf panel.
description: A rectangular cast-iron drainage grate installed alongside a gravel and stone border walkway. The grate features standard vertical drainage slots framed by stylized wavy horizontal accent lines at the top and bottom edges. Integrated into the center of the grate profile is a solid square relief panel beautifully depicting a sprig of three Japanese maple leaves (Momiji).
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kyoto--drain--01.jpg'. A highly aesthetic structural treatment combining standard stormwater runoff function with Kyoto's classic seasonal foliage motifs.

short_description: Painted commemorative Kyoto Student Festival cover.
description: A colorized circular cover dedicated to the Kyoto Student Festival. The multi-colored graphic features a prominent pink-purple torii gate backed by traditional stylized horizontal white clouds. In the foreground, a modern student guitarist stands next to a dynamic traditional dancer performing in front of golden-yellow panels filled with stylized cranes and chrysanthemums. Bold black kanji along the top clouds reads '京都学生祭典' (Kyoto Student Festival).
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kyoto--festival--01.jpg'. This specialty design honors the massive annual festival organized entirely by Kyoto's massive university student population.

short_description: Rectangular fire hydrant lid with orange tread.
description: A compact rectangular cast-iron fire utility cover set flush into asphalt. The central section features a vertical unpainted title plate bearing the official Kyoto City wheel crest at the top, followed by large, deep-cast kanji reading '消火栓' (Fire Hydrant). This central column is flanked on both sides by high-visibility, skid-resistant safety panels painted a bright textured orange with a raised diamond-point tread configuration.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kyoto--fire--01.jpg'. A common municipal emergency asset design emphasizing roadside visibility and quick access for emergency crews.

short_description: Vintage unpainted utility cover with city crest.
description: A heavy-duty, weathered circular cast-iron utility cover set flush in asphalt. The surface is partitioned by raised thick bars into concentric rings, further divided into radiating wedge segments. Each segment contains either a raised hollow circular ring motif or a drainage puncture hole, alternating in a functional pattern. Dead-centered is a classic early variant of the Kyoto City six-pointed star-shaped emblem.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kyoto--industrial--01.jpg'. This shows a classic mid-century municipal design structure prioritizing efficient drainage and traction over pure aesthetics.

short_description: Cast-iron with repeating micro-pattern grid.
description: An unpainted circular cast-iron cover surrounded by standard black aggregate pavement. The high-traction pattern consists of dozens of miniature, repeating concentric gear- or wheel-like medallions densely packed across the face. At the exact axis is a smooth raised circular ring enclosing the prominent Kyoto six-pointed star crest. A single utility notch breaks the bottom border next to a small blank rectangular label box.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kyoto--industrial--02.jpg'. An excellent example of standard anti-slip surface engineering utilizing a dense macro-micro layout pattern.

short_description: Utility lighting cover with fretwork and cherry blossoms.
description: A beautifully cast circular iron utility access hatch enclosed in a clean concrete collar. The background features a dense, geometric triangular mesh or basketweave fretwork pattern. Overlaid gracefully on top of this structure are naturalistic renderings of cherry blossom flowers (Sakura) of varying sizes, concentrated heavily along the left and upper right margins as if drifting across the face. The center medallion holds the six-pointed city crest above clear kanji reading '照明' (Shomei - Lighting).
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kyoto--industrial--03.jpg'. A highly elegant municipal adaptation showing how industrial lighting utilities can blend beautifully with traditional cultural symbols.

short_description: Kyoto Prefectural wastewater cover with honeycomb texture.
description: A dark cast-iron circular utility cover set into a river-pebble aggregate stone path. The field pattern is an immaculate, continuous geometric honeycomb mesh structure. A circular medallion in the core contains the stylized six-petaled chrysanthemum/leaf hybrid emblem representing Kyoto Prefecture. Arced text frames the center; the top banner reads '京都府' (Kyoto Prefecture) and the bottom reads '汚水' (Osui - Wastewater).
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kyoto--industrial--04.jpg'. Highlights the administrative distinction between Kyoto City infrastructure and Kyoto Prefectural jurisdiction using specialized crest layouts.

short_description: Telecommunications cover with repeating cross grid.
description: A dark circular utility access lid bordered by aggregate masonry. The surface profile is composed of an interlocking grid of geometric Greek crosses and square boxes, stamped near the upper center with 'T-25' indicating heavy vehicle load rating. The center features a corporate or utility logo consisting of a stylized swirling emblem within a gear ring. A bottom arched title text banner clearly denotes '通信' (Tsushin - Telecommunications).
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kyoto--industrial--05.jpg'. A heavy-duty, high-load industrial utility hatch dedicated to subsurface communication or fiber line networks.

short_description: Heavy-duty fire cistern cover with concentric segments.
description: A thick, heavily structured circular cast-iron emergency access cover set into a smooth concrete rim. Similar to historical grid layouts, it features a central six-pointed city crest ringed by alternating wedge segments containing drainage punch holes and hollow circles. The wide outer ring contains structural handle notches flanked by massive, deeply embossed kanji character labels reading '防火水槽' (Boka Suiso - Fire Prevention Water Cistern) along the top arch and '京都市消防局' (Kyoto-shi Shobokyoku - Kyoto City Fire Department) on the bottom.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kyoto--industrial--06.jpg'. Historically placed on temple grounds, historical districts, and public squares to access dedicated fire suppression water reservoirs.

short_description: Kyoto City waterworks cover with faux-brick pattern.
description: A circular cast-iron utility cap set flush in textured pavement. The unique background pattern mimics stacked horizontal running-bond masonry bricks. The center features a large version of the old round Kyoto City corporate wheel logo. Arcuate text plaques frame the top and bottom edges; the upper banner reads '京都市指定' (Designated by Kyoto City) while the lower reads in clean western alphabet characters 'SUIKYO' (signifying the Kyoto City Waterworks Bureau).
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kyoto--industrial--07.jpg'. Displays an appealing blend of western alphabet typography alongside classic industrial faux-brick textures.

short_description: Electrical utility with high-traction basketweave pattern.
description: An unpainted circular cast-iron electrical line hatch surrounded by a stone paving ring. The high-friction surface is defined by a standard repeating basketweave or alternating horizontal-vertical block tread layout. Two decorative decorative crest frames flank the left and right center. The centerpiece is a circular medallion containing the six-pointed Kyoto star crest, flanked internally by small kanji characters indicating '関西電力' (Kansai Electric Power) or '関西電化' utilities.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kyoto--industrial--08.jpg'. This generic texture balances high structural traction for pedestrian safety over underground electrical transmission routes.

short_description: Stone-infilled utility cover matching historic flagstone paving.
description: A highly specialized disguised or recessed circular utility hatch embedded into a traditional historic stone walkway. The iron frame of the circle is divided internally into structural paving blocks, which have been painstakingly infilled with textured, rough gray granite flagstone paste to seamlessly camouflage with the surrounding pavement environment. Dead-center is a small square bronze utility plaque stamped with 'C.C.B.' (Common Utility Duct) surrounding a central star-spoke municipal/utility logo.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kyoto--industrial--09.jpg'. Often utilized across highly preserved cultural heritage zones in Kyoto to minimize visual pollution from necessary modern infrastructure.

short_description: Recessed stone-paved frame for combined sewer flows.
description: A recessed circular cast-iron utility collar designed to accommodate flagstone paving infill to match the surrounding stone plaza. Unlike a full metal plate, the central field exposes the natural gray masonry pavement blocks beneath it. The upper crescent portion of the metal ring remains exposed, containing a raised six-pointed Kyoto city star crest to the left, and an integrated raised title box to the right reading '合流' (Goryu - Combined Sewer System).
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kyoto--industrial--10.jpg'. Indicates a shared utility line managing both domestic wastewater and surface stormwater runoff synchronously.

short_description: Recessed stone-paved cover blending into pedestrian plaza.
description: A circular cast-iron frame embedded seamlessly into a multicolored granite paving grid. The interior of the cover is filled with matching rectangular stone tiles to disguise its presence. The central metal hub features the raised six-pointed Kyoto city star crest, and a prominent rectangular plaque at the bottom reads '合流' (Goryu), denoting a combined stormwater and sewage system.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kyoto--industrial--11.jpg'. This design preserves the visual continuity of high-end pedestrian walkways in historic or commercial zones.

short_description: Classic drainage sump cover with arabesque border.
description: An unpainted circular cast-iron utility cover framed by a smooth granite ring. The outer perimeter is decorated with a deeply cast, flowing arabesque or scrollwork pattern. The recessed inner section features several drainage release holes and raised kanji characters arranged circularly, including '排水溜枡' (Haasui Tamemasu), indicating its function as a drainage sump or catch basin.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kyoto--industrial--12.jpg'. A beautiful example of blending decorative traditional scroll aesthetics with utilitarian water management hardware.

short_description: High-traction utility cover with dense hexagonal pattern.
description: A dark, circular cast-iron cover set flush in brushed concrete. The entire face is covered in a highly dense anti-slip pattern composed of miniature raised dots clustered into interlocking hexagonal rosettes. A small six-pointed Kyoto city crest sits dead-center. Two text tabs are integrated along the upper rim: the right side reads 'きょうとし' (Kyoto-shi) and the left reads 'ごうりゅう' (Goryu - Combined Sewer).
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kyoto--industrial--13.jpg'. Optimized for pedestrian safety and maximum traction while displaying standard municipal branding in hiragana.

short_description: Official PokéFuta cover featuring Cyndaquil and Darmanitan.
description: A vibrant, fully painted collaborative Pokémon cover ('Pokéfuta') set into a gravel-aggregate walkway. The design depicts Cyndaquil (ヒノアラシ) looking upward on a stone path next to a fiery Darmanitan (ヒヒダルma) framed by stone temple lanterns and stylized greenery. The beige outer border contains the character names in katakana, 'きょうと' (Kyoto) in hiragana, and the official corporate copyright stamp.
artist: Nintendo / Creatures Inc. / GAME FREAK inc.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kyoto--pokeman--01.jpg'. Part of Japan's country-wide tourism initiative linking regional discovery with iconic pop-culture designs.

short_description: Official PokéFuta cover featuring Legendary Pokémon Ho-Oh.
description: A colorful painted Pokéfuta utility lid showcasing the Legendary Pokémon Ho-Oh (ホウオウ) soaring majestically across a golden sky. In the background, a traditional five-story Japanese pagoda and rolling green mountains pay homage to Kyoto's iconic historical landscape. The dark outer frame carries the name typography and regional destination label.
artist: Nintendo / Creatures Inc. / GAME FREAK inc.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kyoto--pokeman--02.jpg'. Ho-Oh is a highly fitting choice for Kyoto, drawing direct thematic inspiration from the Johto region's traditional aesthetic.

short_description: PokéFuta cover with Pichu, Cleffa, and Igglybuff.
description: A beautifully painted circular Pokémon utility hatch showing baby Pokémon Pichu (ピチュウ), Cleffa (ピィ), and Igglybuff (ププリン) joyfully playing around a blooming pink cherry blossom (sakura) tree. The pastoral scene is set against a bright blue sky backdrop, surrounded by a clean cream-colored border listing the featured characters and 'きょうと'.
artist: Nintendo / Creatures Inc. / GAME FREAK inc.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kyoto--pokeman--03.jpg'. A soft, cheerful seasonal display highlighting spring flora alongside popular franchise mascots.

short_description: Painted waterworks cover featuring cartoon firefly mascots.
description: A highly colorful municipal utility cover displaying two cheerful cartoon firefly mascots dressed with bowties, flying amidst bright pink azalea blossoms. Hanging down from the top are elegant clusters of purple wisteria branches. The top left corner displays the welcoming Kyoto greeting 'おこしやす' (Okoshiyasu), while the bottom text reads '京都市 上下水道局' (Kyoto City Water and Sewerage Bureau).
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kyoto--pokeman--04.jpg'. This design highlights the clean water required for fireflies to thrive, symbolizing the environmental success of the local water system.

short_description: Bronze directional street marker for Teramachi intersection.
description: A square, polished bronze geographical orientation plate embedded into paving stones. The center features a detailed 16-point nautical compass rose indicating North. The four directional axes explicitly label the intersecting pathways in both kanji and English: '寺町通' (Teramachi-dori) and '錦小路通' (Nishikikoji-dori), equipped with directional arrow pointers. The corners are adorned with relief carvings of morning glory vines.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kyoto--street-plate--01.jpg'. Placed directly in the pavement at major pedestrian cross-points to assist tourists navigating the market grid.

short_description: Square vintage sewer cap with municipal branding.
description: A heavy square cast-iron utility access plate sitting in dirt and ground debris. The central area features a raised circular ring motif framing an early iteration of the municipal waterworks insignia—a six-spoked variant wheel. Below the central emblem, two bold, raised kanji characters state '下水' (Gesui), marking it directly as a subsurface sewage line node.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-kyoto--street-plate--02.jpg'. A rugged, old-school industrial layout that emphasizes pure utility and longevity over modern cosmetic paint styles.

short_description: Clean geometric radial circle pattern cover.
description: An unpainted, cast-iron cover featuring an intricate geometric layout of concentric and interlocking circles radiating outwards from a central multi-pointed star or wheel motif. Includes a small rectangular text block on the left with the hiragana 'おすい' (osui), indicating wastewater.
notes: The text reads 'おすい' (Wastewater). The design is clean and abstract compared to regional pictorial covers.

short_description: Colorized cover featuring Sika deer and cherry blossoms.
description: A beautifully detailed, colorized cover depicting Nara's famous Sika deer standing amidst blooming cherry blossoms (sakura). The central crest features the traditional emblem of Nara City surrounded by a floral border. A small label at the bottom reads '汚水' (osui) for wastewater.
notes: Features the classic symbols of Nara: the sacred Sika deer and sakura. The text at the bottom reads '汚水' (Wastewater).

short_description: Rectangular fire hydrant lid with diamond tread pattern.
description: A rectangular, dark iron utility lid built into a painted yellow frame. The lid face features a textured diamond grip pattern, two recessed lifting handles, and the Nara City emblem in the center. The text markings read '奈良市' (Nara City) and '消火栓' (Fire Hydrant). The painted frame above reads '駐車禁止' (No Parking).
notes: The orientation of the text on the lid reads '奈良市' (Nara City) on top and '消火栓' (Fire Hydrant) on the bottom. The curb stencil warns '駐車禁止' (No Parking).

short_description: Geometric patterned cover with gold center emblem.
description: A circular cover featuring a radial geometric pattern filled with a reddish-brown background paint. The standout feature is a highly detailed, golden-colored Nara City emblem in the exact center. The lower section includes the silver raised-text kanji '汚水' (osui), identifying it as a wastewater line.
notes: The central gold-painted insignia is the official city emblem of Nara. The lower text reads '汚水' (Wastewater).

short_description: Colorized Tennoji Ward variant with Osaka Castle design.
description: A light-blue, pink, and white painted variant of the iconic Osaka Castle cover. It depicts the majestic multi-tiered keep of Osaka Castle flanked by blooming cherry blossoms (sakura) and dynamic cresting water waves at the base. The top features the standard stylized Osaka City crest, while the bottom-left explicitly lists the localized area text '天王寺区' (Tennoji Ward).
notes: A ward-specific edition of the classic municipal design. The text reads '天王寺区' (Tennoji Ward).

short_description: Commemorative 125th Anniversary Osaka City Sewerage cover.
description: A heavy industrial iron cover honoring a municipal milestone. The inner medallion displays a stylized split scene: modern skyscrapers on the left and a geometric, block-art rendering of Osaka Castle on the right. The text reads '大阪市下水道事業 125周年' (Osaka City Sewerage Project 125th Anniversary). The traditional 'Mio-tsukushi' city mark is placed in a badge at the bottom-left and inside the top number 25.
notes: Issued to commemorate the 125th anniversary of the Osaka City Sewerage system, established originally in 1894. Some specialty versions of this award-winning public design were installed to illuminate at night.

short_description: Unpainted landscape cover showcasing historic and modern landmarks.
description: A dense, unpainted pictorial cast-iron cover illustrating Osaka's landscape alongside the Yodo River. The design includes Osaka Castle, the OBP Twin Towers, a floating water bus (Suijou-Bus), and a multi-arched bridge. The outer rim is bordered by a ring of stylized cherry blossoms and willow leaves. The outer frame text reads '大阪市 水道' (Osaka City Waterworks) and '制水弁' (Flow Control Valve).
notes: The bottom text reads '制水弁' (Seisuiben - Flow Control Valve or Sluice Valve), indicating its technical utility type.

short_description: Recycled sludge plaque featuring regional Osaka-ben phrases.
description: A rectangular public sidewalk marker cast in a bronze/brass metallic style. The upper text translates to: 'This brick is made from recycled sewage sludge.' The main body of the plaque is a dense typographic text collage written in the local Osaka dialect (Osaka-ben), including famous phrases like 'まいど' (Maido), 'ぼちぼchidenna' (Bochi-bochi denna), and '好っきゃねん' (Sukkyanen). The bottom right is stamped '大阪市' (Osaka City).
notes: An eco-friendly municipal plaque that turns treated wastewater sludge into public street art while celebrating the regional dialect.

short_description: Yellow fire hydrant cover featuring ladder truck design.
description: A high-visibility circular utility cover painted in vibrant safety yellow with a textured dimple grip surface. The central relief graphic shows a red fire engine equipped with a deployable extension ladder. Red banner text flanking the truck reads '消火栓' (Fire Hydrant) and '駐車禁止' (No Parking), along with a banner reading '火の用心' (Beware of Fire) raised on the ladder.
notes: The English 'OSAKA CITY' is molded along the top edge, framing the historic 'Mio-tsukushi' city emblem. The central labels read '消火栓' (Fire Hydrant) and '駐車禁止' (No Parking).

short_description: Water valve cover with a dramatic splash design.
description: An unpainted, circular iron cover featuring a heavy textured grid pattern in the upper half and a stylized water geyser or splash in the lower half. The central icon resembles a historic water key or hydrant valve. The text '水道' (suido - waterworks) and '水' (mizu - water) surround the small Osaka City water emblem at the bottom.
notes: The text reads '水道' (Waterworks) and '水' (Water). It features the distinct inverted double-arch water drop insignia of the Osaka City Waterworks Bureau.

short_description: Cast iron cover with vibrant blue outer ring.
description: A circular utility cover divided into two main sections: a bright blue painted outer border punctuated by dark pebble-like shapes, and a central cast-iron circle filled with tightly repeating horizontal wave patterns. The center features a stylized logo with the text '仕切弁' (Sikiriben/Shut-off valve) and the Otsu city emblem underneath.
notes: The text reads '仕切弁', indicating a control or shut-off valve. The design heavily leans into water motifs, likely paying homage to Lake Biwa.

short_description: Unpainted cover celebrating notable Lake Biwa landmarks.
description: A highly detailed, unpainted cast-iron cover that compresses several iconic Otsu sightseeing spots into one scene. It shows the Biwako Flower Fountain shooting water arcs, the historic 'Michigan' paddle-wheel cruise ship, sailboats, a crew rowing team, a bird flying over the Biwako Ohashi Bridge, and massive fireworks overhead. The Otsu City emblem sits inside a square block at the bottom center, framed by cherry blossoms and bird-foot violets.
notes: This design is the standard artistic layout for Otsu City, packing almost all major local tourism symbols into a single relief mold.

short_description: Colorized Gundam cover featuring Otsu-e style Oni.
description: A special edition, colorized promotional cover featuring the head of the classic RX-78-2 Mobile Suit Gundam. Standing right next to it is a traditional folk art character—a demon dressed in Buddhist monk robes beating a gong—rendered in the classic local 'Otsu-e' painting style. The labels read 'OTSU', 'RX-78-2 GUNDAM', '大津絵' (Otsu-e), and '鬼の念仏' (Oni no Nenbutsu).
foundry: Kunio Okawara (Gundam designer) / Traditional Otsu-e motifs
notes: Part of the official 'Gundam Manhole Project' collaboration, explicitly pairing iconic anime with local folk history. Marked with '©SOTSU・SUNRISE'.

short_description: Colorized landscape cover featuring light blue waters.
description: A beautifully painted variant of Otsu's landmark cover. The water elements of Lake Biwa are filled with soft sky-blue paint, and details like the Biwako Flower Fountain, fireworks, and sailboats are highlighted in white, yellow, green, and red. The bottom features an 'OTSU 100' anniversary logo next to a cartoon mascot character.
notes: Includes the 'OTSU 100' commemorative stamp at the bottom, celebrating the centennial of Otsu's municipal status.

short_description: Decorative tile mosaic map of Lake Biwa.
description: A large, multi-tile circular installation embedded in a public walkway. It features a sharp, dark blue eight-pointed compass rose tracking North, East, South, and West. Laid directly over the center is a bright light-blue silhouette map of Lake Biwa, Shiga's central geographic feature. The text proudly reads 'LAKE BIWA' and 'OTSU' over a lime-green ring background, accompanied by the orange Otsu city mark.
notes: Not a utility hatch cover, but an architectural sidewalk mosaic plaque charting Lake Biwa's shape and orientation relative to Otsu.

short_description: PokéLid cover featuring Gyarados and Shiny Gyarados.
description: An official, fully colorized collaborative 'PokéLid' utility cover. The artwork dynamically illustrates two giant serpentine Pokémon: a standard blue Gyarados crashing through the surf alongside a rare, red 'Shiny' Gyarados. The top-right edge reads 'ギャラドス' (Gyarados), the lower left reads 'おおつ' (Otsu) in hiragana, and the bottom center bears the official '©Pokémon' trademark logo.
notes: Part of Japan's national PokéLids (ポケふた) installation project. Gyarados is a fitting choice given Otsu's status as the gateway to Japan's largest freshwater lake.

short_description: Concentric wave patterned cover with star emblem.
description: A heavy-duty, unpainted cast-iron circular utility lid. The surface pattern features deep, concentric radiating rings of sharp, zigzagging wave lines, broken up evenly by small circular ventilation/drainage punch holes. The exact center features the classic Otsu City crest (a five-pointed star cradled inside a stylized circular crescent loop).
notes: A functional, non-colorized industrial design prioritizing drainage slip-resistance while retaining the signature water-wave motif of the region.

short_description: Cover depicting traditional gassho-zukuri houses.
description: A circular cast-iron utility cover featuring a detailed pictorial landscape of Shirakawa-go. The lower right portion proudly illustrates three iconic traditional thatched-roof 'gassho-zukuri' farmhouses nestled together. To the left, stylized rounded mountain peaks rise toward a textured sky filled with swirling horizontal cloud patterns. Positioned near the center-top, bold, organic kanji and hiragana script reads 'しらかわごう 白川郷' (Shirakawa-go).
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-shirakawa-go--houses--01.jpg'. This beautiful unpainted design perfectly showcases the UNESCO World Heritage site's famous historic architecture.

short_description: Circular drainage grate with city crest.
description: A circular cast-iron drainage grate section installed flush in the pavement. The layout features wide horizontal drainage slots divided by a central vertical bar and a decorative square border border. Dead-center is the stylized municipal crest of Takayama City, featuring a diamond-like geometric design. The outer rim is embellished with a dual-row repeating square micro-tread pattern.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-takayama--drain--01.jpg'. Designed specifically to manage heavy stormwater runoff efficiently while ensuring pedestrian traction along the outer rim.

short_description: Unpainted utility cover with rhododendron design.
description: A dark, unpainted circular utility cover showcasing a dense pictorial relief of blooming rhododendron flowers, the official city flower of Takayama. The surface is completely covered in multi-petaled blossoms, pointed leaves, and budding branches. Integrated into the bottom border is the stylized kanji for sewerage, reading '下水' (Gesui).
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-takayama--flowers--01.jpg'. A beautifully textured municipal piece that celebrates local alpine flora across the city's standard drainage network.

short_description: Textured lid showing stylized rhododendron blossoms.
description: A circular weathered cast-iron utility hatch enclosed within a concrete support collar. The centerpiece features a naturalistic relief layout of two prominent rhododendron flowers growing upward from a cluster of thick, veined leaves. Two rectangular integrated lifting hook pockets break the graphic symmetrically on the left and right sides.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-takayama--flowers--02.jpg'. A highly structured variation of the city's signature floral motif, optimizing space around structural utility mechanics.

short_description: Circular iron plate with alpine rhododendrons.
description: A clean, unpainted circular iron utility access plate set into a concrete masonry frame. The artistic layout depicts a pair of large alpine rhododendron blossoms intertwined with wide, deeply ribbed leaves. The composition fills the entire round frame, utilizing contrasting textures between the smooth petals and the raised leaf veins.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-takayama--flowers--03.jpg'. This presents a slightly modified close-up casting variation of Takayama's standard regional botanical design.

short_description: Vibrantly painted rhododendron city utility cover.
description: A beautiful, colorized version of Takayama's signature municipal design set into stone paving. The relief features brightly painted pink rhododendron blossoms with soft white accents, surrounded by rich, muted green leaves. Two square text inserts are cast on opposite ends: the left reads '下' (Gesu) and the bottom reads '水' (Sui), indicating wastewater service.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-takayama--flowers-color--01.jpg'. This painted variant is meticulously maintained to provide a vibrant pop of color in high-end pedestrian and historic shopping districts.

short_description: Industrial honeycomb cover with city crest.
description: A heavy-duty circular industrial utility plate with a wet, reflective metallic face. The field pattern is composed of a continuous geometric honeycomb grid of interlocking hexagons. Positioned exactly at the center is a small circular medallion bearing the geometric, stylized crest of Takayama City. Two vertical rectangular lifting slots flank the central emblem.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-takayama--industrial--01.jpg'. Built to handle substantial structural loads while minimizing slip hazards through a geometric tread layout.

short_description: Textured traction lid with municipal seal.
description: An unpainted circular cast-iron utility access lid framed by brushed concrete. The anti-slip surface texture is made up of dozens of small, repeating circular dimples tightly packed together. In the center is a gear-edged medallion framing the geometric Takayama city symbol. A lower arched plaque contains deep-set kanji reading '下水' (Gesui), denoting sewage utility access.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-takayama--industrial--02.jpg'. A classic utilitarian layout focusing on maximum foot traffic safety and distinct structural function markings.

short_description: Official PokéFuta cover featuring elemental monkeys.
description: A highly colorful, officially painted collaborative Pokémon 'Pokéfuta' utility lid embedded in granite flagstones. The vibrant cartoon scene depicts the three elemental monkey Pokémon: the green Pansage (ヤナップ), the red Pansear (バオップ), and the blue Panpour (ヒヤップ) leaping playfully. In the background, a traditional wooden building and a red bridge hint at Takayama's famous Nakabashi Bridge. The white outer ring displays the character names in katakana, 'たかやま' (Takayama) in hiragana, and the corporate copyright stamp.
artist: Nintendo / Creatures Inc. / GAME FREAK inc.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-takayama--pokeman--01.jpg'. An iconic tourist feature seamlessly merging popular pop culture with landmarks unique to Takayama's heritage.

short_description: Circular utility cover featuring a stylized bird medallion.
description: An unpainted circular cast-iron utility plate set flush within a textured concrete ring. The main field is textured with a dense, repeating geometric key-fret or maze pattern to maximize pedestrian traction. At the absolute center is a circular relief medallion displaying a highly stylized, front-facing bird motif with large circular eyes and prominent curved plumage. The bottom margin is stamped with a manufacturer banner reading 'FUKUNISHI' next to a diamond logo, while the upper rim features cast technical text reading '600-5000K 20'.
notes: Located near the Tsukiji Hongwan-ji Temple. This artistic lid belongs to a coordinated wildlife series, combining heavy structural utility with custom local iconography.

short_description: Rectangular bronze-toned drainage grate with interlocking arabesque patterns.
description: An elegant, heavy-duty rectangular openwork trench drain section positioned flush against polished granite flagstones and a small wall drain inlet. The functional surface features a highly decorative, symmetrical layout of interlocking scrollwork and traditional flowing arabesque open loops designed to let surface water drain freely. The outer upper and lower metal borders are accented by a matching row of small repeating rectangular indentations showing a slight greenish patina.
notes: Located near the Tsukiji Hongwan-ji Temple. A premium architectural drainage treatment frequently utilized near high-profile plazas or historical complexes to elegantly mask modern stormwater infrastructure.

short_description: Circular utility cover featuring a stylized elephant medallion.
description: An unpainted circular cast-iron utility access lid surrounded by a stone-gray pavement collar. It utilizes the exact same casting template as its companion bird piece, including the dense anti-slip geometric key-fret background pattern, side handle slots, and the lower 'FUKUNISHI' foundry mark. The central medallion instead features a beautiful, stylized relief of an elephant facing forward with its trunk sweeping gracefully to the left side.
notes: Located near the Tsukiji Hongwan-ji Temple. Pairs directly with the bird layout design, highlighting a themed municipal deployment of custom-cast infrastructural hardware.

short_description: Yellow fire hydrant cover depicting historic firefighters.
description: A rectangular, high-visibility cast-iron cover painted safety yellow with a dotted pattern rim. The inner rectangular plate is colorfully painted and depicts two historic Edo-period firefighters (hikeshi) in traditional protective robes and helmets working together, with a classic townscape in the background. The text along the top reads '消火栓' (Fire Hydrant) inside circular markers, and the traditional Tokyo Metropolitan crest is featured in the top right corner.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-tokyo--fire--01.jpg'. A classic, widespread design across Tokyo highlighting the metropolis's historical firefighting culture.

short_description: Classic unpainted Tokyo municipal cherry blossom design.
description: The quintessential unpainted Tokyo Metropolitan sewer cover. The design features a prominent, symmetrical five-petal Somei-Yoshino cherry blossom with textured perforations inside the petals for drainage and traction. Delicate ginkgo leaves curve elegantly between the main petals. A rectangular plate at the bottom explicitly marks its network purpose: '合流' (Combined Sewer).
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-tokyo--flower--01.jpg'. This iconic design has been an enduring public design staple across the capital for decades.

short_description: Standard Tokyo utility cover featuring a geometric cherry blossom lattice.
description: A classic, unpainted circular cast-iron utility plate set flush into an asphalt street with a bold white painted diagonal traffic line. The surface texture is defined by a dense arrangement of small anti-slip traction nubs that trace an abstract, interlocking geometric pattern suggesting stylized cherry blossom outlines. The very center features a rectangular data plaque reading '東京下水道' (Tokyo Sewerage) above a line of four numbered, color-coded plug indicators (orange, green, teal, and beige) marking technical specification data, flanked by '管布設' (Pipe Laid) and the year indicator. The bottom plate section displays the text '合流' (Combined System).
notes: A ubiquitous and iconic industrial casting found throughout the municipal wards of Tokyo, beautifully blending high-traction infrastructure engineering with subtle regional sakura geometry.

short_description: Tokyo municipal utility cover with stylized cherry blossom and ginkgo motifs.
description: An unpainted circular cast-iron utility access lid displaying a clean, stylized variant of Tokyo's regional flora. The prominent central design features a mirrored layout of abstract cherry blossoms and structural ginkgo patterns, accented by textured sections embedded with small raised circles to provide pedestrian grip. A central horizontal nameplate reads '東京下水道' (Tokyo Sewerage) above four colored, hexagonal technical plugs (yellow, green, teal, and beige) and the characters for '管布設' (Pipe Laid). The lower segment is cast with the classification mark '合流' (Combined System).
notes: Features a prominent variant of the classic Tokyo Sewerage Bureau infrastructure design, beautifully combining the cherry blossom (the prefectural flower) and the ginkgo leaf (the prefectural tree) symbols into a high-traction architectural casting.

short_description: Intricate relief utility cover showing sweeping cherry blossom branches.
description: A wet, unpainted circular cast-iron utility cover set flush into textured granite flagstone paving. The entire field is treated as a detailed artistic canvas, featuring a deeply cast relief of blooming cherry blossom branches extending from the top left, with individual delicate petals caught in a gust of wind and drifting across the right-side negative space. The lower margin features a recessed plaque bearing the stylized ginkgo leaf emblem of Tokyo Prefecture alongside the cast text '低圧' (Low Pressure), indicating its placement over a low-pressure utility or gas line.
notes: A highly aesthetic, unpainted technical lid variant that captures a classic Japanese spring scene with exceptional textural detail, utilizing deep relief grooves to aid surface drainage.

short_description: Tokyo Metropolitan Park utility cover depicting cherry blossoms and a flying pigeon.
description: An unpainted circular utility lid embedded within a square concrete foundation ring, surrounded by lush green clover grass and a rustic wooden barrier post. The design features a beautifully fluid, cast relief of cherry blossom flowers sweeping across the upper left field, while a large, stylized pigeon or dove glides gracefully through the lower right field amidst stylized wind currents. Two rectangular indentation slots flank the centerpiece for structural lifting. The top rim is stamped with '大正' (Taisho) or standard utility indicators, while the prominent lower border reads '都立公園' (Metropolitan Park).
notes: A specialized custom casting exclusively deployed within public green spaces managed by the Tokyo Metropolitan Government, highlighting park wildlife and seasonal sakura blooms.

short_description: Color-printed Shinjuku pop culture cover featuring Godzilla vs. Mothra.
description: A specialty circular utility cover featuring a striking, full-color graphic display insert protected by a heavy iron outer frame. The outer frame is detailed with concentric anti-slip dimples and standard municipal text reading '東京下水道' (Tokyo Sewerage) and '合流' (Combined System). The main graphic depicts the legendary King of the Monsters, Godzilla, roaring amidst a nighttime Tokyo cityscape under dramatic blue searchlights, while the giant divine moth, Mothra, soars through the sky behind him. The bold white text '新宿' (Shinjuku) is printed across the lower foreground, accompanied by a small Toho licensing copyright stamp.
notes: Installed in Shinjuku's Kabukicho district near the Shinjuku Toho Building, this commemorative piece celebrates Godzilla's official status as a tourism ambassador for Shinjuku City and depicts scenes inspired by the 1992 film 'Godzilla vs. Mothra'.

short_description: Metallic relief pop culture cover showing a pink-backed Godzilla silhouette.
description: A commemorative circular cast-iron utility cover featuring a high-contrast artistic layout. The outer border is detailed with small traction rings and houses the text '東京下水道' (Tokyo Sewerage) and '合流' (Combined System). The main field features a striking metallic profile silhouette of Godzilla's iconic jagged back spines, tail, and head crashing through beams of light. The negative spaces of the cutout design are filled with a textured, vibrant salmon-pink background. On the left side, stylized characters spell out '新宿' (Shinjuku), while the bottom margin features a prominent raised copyright text reading '©TOHO CO., LTD.'
notes: Located in Shinjuku's bustling entertainment hub near Tokyu Kabukicho Tower, this sleek silhouette variant honors the 2016 film 'Shin Godzilla' and pays homage to Shinjuku's deep cinematic ties with the franchise.

short_description: Ukiyo-e tribute cover featuring Hokusai's Great Wave.
description: A striking circular cover featuring a colored, detailed insert of Katsushika Hokusai's world-famous woodblock print, 'The Great Wave off Kanagawa', displaying cresting blue waves with white sea foam and Mount Fuji in the background. The stylized hiragana text 'すみだ' (Sumida) is printed on the sky background. The outer cast-iron frame has a wave-like scale pattern and features text reading '東京下水道' (Tokyo Sewerage) and '雨水' (Stormwater).
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-tokyo--great-wave--01.jpg'. This public art installation celebrates Sumida Ward as the birthplace of the legendary ukiyo-e master Hokusai.

short_description: Square reddish-brown utility cover featuring the faithful dog Hachiko.
description: A square, flush-mounted utility access hatch set into a cobblestone sidewalk next to asphalt paving. The plate is coated in a textured, reddish-brown/salmon protective paint layer. The artistic layout depicts a bold, stylized relief silhouette of Tokyo's famously loyal Akita dog, Hachiko, sitting alertly and facing right. Leafy branches frame the upper corners of the scene, and two rectangular lifting hook recesses are integrated symmetrically on the left and right sides of the main figure.
notes: Located right at the edge of Shibuya Crossing near Shibuya Station. This custom piece pays homage to the legendary story of Hachiko, serving as a subtle structural landmark in one of Tokyo's most heavily foot-trafficked districts.

short_description: Diamond tread pattern cover for drainage systems.
description: An unpainted, industrial circular utility cover featuring a continuous diamond tread pattern across its entire surface to provide traction. The upper center has a small hexagonal frame with the kanji '水' (Water). The exact center features a horizontal rectangular nameplate reading '排水室' (Drainage Room / Discharging Chamber), and the bottom has a matching single kanji frame reading '道' (Way/Road). Two circular municipal marks flank the central block.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-tokyo--industrial--02.jpg'.

short_description: Industrial electrical utility cover with basketweave texture.
description: An industrial cast-iron circular utility cover with a highly dense, interlocking basketweave or cross-hatch slip-resistant texture. The center features a distinct circular emblem enclosing the single kanji '電' (Den - Electricity). Molded along the lower bottom edge is the manufacturing company name 'DAIICHIKIZAI CO., LTD.'
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-tokyo--industrial--03.jpg'. Produced by Daiichikizai, a prominent Tokyo-based architectural and civil engineering hardware firm.

short_description: Unpainted industrial wastewater cover with traction grid.
description: A flat, unpainted cast-iron circular cover featuring an elongated diamond mesh grid pattern for heavy-duty skid prevention. The exact center features a small circular emblem housing the two kanji '汚水' (Osui - Wastewater / Sewage). The lower rim contains technical manufacturing serial stamps 'MGDHG 300 RC SC'.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-tokyo--industrial--04.jpg'. A utility-focused asset optimized for public roads and sidewalks.

short_description: Ueno Park Poké Lid featuring Baltoy and Bronzor with ancient artifact motifs.
description: A vibrant, full-color circular Pokéfuta (Pokémon manhole cover) set flush into dark asphalt. The outer iron border frames a highly detailed, lime-green and olive-green graphic field. The symmetrical layout features four Baltoy (ヤジロン) arranged cardinally, facing inward toward a central, blue Bronzor (ドーミラー) motif. Interspersed between the characters are stylized depictions of ancient leaves, geometric shapes, and classic Poké Balls. The top curved margin displays the Pokémon names in green katakana, 'ヤジロン' and 'ドーミラー', while the bottom margin features the location name 'うえの' (Ueno) in hiragana and the official '©Pokémon' copyright text.
notes: Located near the entrance approach of the Tokyo National Museum in Ueno Park. The choice of Baltoy (inspired by Jomon-period clay dogu figures) and Bronzor (resembling ancient bronze mirrors) elegantly honors the archaeological antiquities housed within the adjacent museum.

short_description: Ueno Park Poké Lid showcasing Tyrunt and Wynaut in front of the National Museum of Nature and Science.
description: A beautifully illustrated circular utility cover installed within a light-colored, exposed-aggregate pebble walkway. The design features a bold, bright orange outer border ring containing the katakana text 'チゴラス' (Tyrunt) and 'ソーナノ' (Wynaut) along the top, and 'うえの' (Ueno) alongside '©Pokémon' at the bottom. The central illustration captures a brown, dinosaur-like Tyrunt with bared teeth walking alongside a cheerful, bright-blue Wynaut. The background stylized line art depicts the iconic architectural facade of the National Museum of Nature and Science.
notes: Positioned near the main entrance of the National Museum of Nature and Science in Ueno Park. Tyrunt, a prehistoric 'fossil' Pokémon, serves as a playful nod to the extensive dinosaur skeleton and natural history exhibits housed inside the museum.

short_description: Colorized Minato Ward cover featuring five Guardians.
description: A vibrant anime collector cover featuring a high-contrast graphic print of five main characters from 'Pretty Guardian Sailor Moon' against a brilliant green background, including Sailor Moon, Sailor Mercury, Sailor Mars, Sailor Jupiter, and Sailor Venus. The title text '美少女戦士セーラームーン / Pretty Guardian Sailor Moon' sits at the top with a crescent moon logo. The bottom frame reads '東京下水道' (Tokyo Sewerage) and '合流' (Combined Sewer).
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-tokyo--sailor-moon--01.jpg'. Installed as part of an official tourism project in Minato Ward, the canonical home setting of the series' protagonist.

short_description: Sailor Moon alignment variant under starry background.
description: A beautiful circular anime cover showing full-body graphic portraits of the five classic Sailor Guardians standing confidently under a dreamlike, deep blue starry night sky background with a glowing full moon behind them. The official copyright mark '©Naoko Takeuchi' is visible in the bottom right corner of the insert. The outer iron rim features standard Tokyo Metropolitan geometric detailing.
notes: Referenced in file 'jp-tokyo--sailor-moon--02.jpg'. Another highly sought-after installment of the 2024 Minato Ward pop-culture design rollouts.

short_description: Tokyo Metropolitan Park utility cover depicting a traditional garden landscape with a pine tree and teahouse.
description: An unpainted circular cast-iron utility cover set securely inside a square concrete collar on a dirt and grass path. The beautifully composed pictorial relief features a large, sculptural Japanese black pine tree stretching up the left foreground, framing a traditional waterside teahouse building across a pond. Two structural lifting slots are integrated into the teahouse windows. At the exact center of the landscape scene is a circular medallion showcasing the stylized ginkgo leaf emblem of Tokyo Prefecture. The lower field includes a small cloud or hedge motif containing the characters '低圧' (Low Pressure), surrounded by a patterned depiction of rippling water or stepping stones.
notes: Located within Hamarikyu Gardens. The scenic casting perfectly represents the park's historic Edo-period design, beautifully capturing its famous tidal pond, traditional teahouses, and meticulously manicured pine trees.

short_description: Uji Bridge design framed by maple leaves.
description: This Uji City sewer cover features a stylized view of Uji Bridge, one of the city's best-known landmarks, framed by maple leaves and flowing cloud or river motifs. The design reflects Uji's historic riverside setting and its association with scenic walks, temple visits, and seasonal foliage. The inscription reads 宇治市, meaning Uji City, and おすい, meaning sewage.
notes: This appears to be a common Uji City design found in multiple locations around the city. The bridge motif is likely Uji Bridge over the Uji River.

short_description: Linear drainage grate featuring yellow city flower.
description: This decorative linear street drain in Uji features colored panels of yamabuki, or Japanese kerria, identified on the cover as the city's flower. The yellow blossoms and green leaves are set into a long drainage grate, adding a local botanical motif to an otherwise functional piece of street infrastructure. The central emblem appears to be the Uji City symbol, tying the design to the municipality.
notes: The inscription 市の花 やまぶき means city flower: yamabuki. Yamabuki is commonly known in English as Japanese kerria. This appears to be a decorative linear drain rather than a round drain or utility cover.

short_description: Street drain grate with maple leaf inset.
description: This rectangular Uji street drain combines a functional drainage grate with a small decorative inset panel showing maple leaves. The design is simpler than the larger round municipal covers, but it carries the same seasonal leaf motif seen around the city. The contrast between the utilitarian slotted grate and the small ornamental panel makes it a good example of how even everyday drainage infrastructure in Uji can include local visual identity.
notes: This type of rectangular drain was commonly found around Uji. The small inset panel appears to show maple leaves; the small text on the panel should be checked if a clearer close-up is available.

short_description: Bright yellow fire truck hydrant cover design.
description: This brightly colored Uji utility cover marks access to firefighting water infrastructure. The design shows a red ladder fire truck on a yellow background, making the cover easy to identify in the street. The text 消火栓 means fire hydrant, 駐車禁止 means no parking, and the central 水 character means water.
notes: This design was found in multiple locations around Uji. It is a functional fire hydrant or firefighting water access cover rather than a decorative sewer cover.

short_description: Wastewater cover featuring repeating leaf pattern background.
description: This Uji City sewer cover uses a repeating leaf pattern around the city emblem, creating a simple but distinctive municipal design. The leaf motif fits Uji's identity as a historic tea-growing and tea-culture center, while the overall design remains more abstract and functional than the city's larger scenic covers. The text 汚水 means sewage or wastewater.
notes: This design was commonly found around Uji. The central symbol appears to be the Uji City emblem. The repeated leaves may reference Uji's tea culture, but this should be treated as an interpretation rather than a confirmed design note.

short_description: Distinctive three-tab geometric industrial wastewater cover.
description: This Uji wastewater cover has a distinctive three-tab outer shape and an open hexagonal grate pattern, giving it a more industrial appearance than the city's decorative scenic covers. A small central floral or municipal-style emblem sits above the label 汚水, meaning sewage or wastewater. The design is functional and geometric, but still includes a subtle decorative center detail.
notes: This cover appeared less common than the standard round Uji wastewater covers. The three-tab shape and open hexagonal grate pattern make it visually distinctive. The central symbol may be a floral or municipal-style emblem, but it should be confirmed before describing it more specifically.

short_description: Decorative architectural entryway piece at tea shop.
description: This octagonal metal floor piece was found just outside the door of the famous Nakamura Tokichi Main Branch in Uji. Unlike the municipal drain and utility covers in the collection, this appears to be a decorative architectural detail rather than a functional street cover. Its simple geometric form, dark metal finish, and raised circular studs give the entryway a textured, traditional-meets-industrial character.
notes: This is not a drain or utility cover. It was photographed outside Nakamura Tokichi Main Branch, a well-known tea shop in Uji.

short_description: Pokémon cover located near Asagiri Bridge.
description: This colorful Pokémon-themed decorative cover was found on the east side of Asagiri Bridge in Uji. The design features Scorbunny and Spinda among leafy greenery, with a pathway-like composition that fits the riverside walking area near the bridge. Like other Poké Lid-style covers, it turns a small piece of street infrastructure into a playful local landmark.
notes: One-off decorative Pokémon cover found on the east side of Asagiri Bridge. The Japanese names shown are ヒバニー and パッチール, corresponding to Scorbunny and Spinda.

short_description: Tea-themed Pokémon cover at historical park.
description: This colorful Pokémon-themed cover was found at the Historical Park of Tea and Uji Town. The design features the tea-related Pokémon Sinistcha and Poltchageist, shown among matcha, tea utensils, maple leaves, and a traditional bridge-like backdrop. The cover connects Uji’s famous tea culture with Japan’s popular decorative utility cover tradition, turning a street feature into a playful local landmark.
notes: This appears to be a one-of-a-kind Pokémon decorative cover found at the Historical Park of Tea and Uji Town. The Japanese names shown are ヤバソチャ and チャデス, corresponding to Sinistcha and Poltchageist.

short_description: Small round blue municipal water valve cover.
description: This small Uji City utility cover marks a water-service gate valve or shutoff valve. The blue color, compact round format, and central emblem distinguish it from the larger decorative sewer and storm drain covers found around the city. The text 仕切弁 means gate valve or shutoff valve, while 宇治市 identifies it as belonging to Uji City.
notes: The markings include 仕切弁, meaning gate valve or shutoff valve, and 宇治市, meaning Uji City. The numbers and letters, including 75 and S, may indicate pipe size, valve type, or maintenance classification.

short_description: Yokohama cover depicting Bay Bridge inside a ship wheel.
description: An unpainted circular cast-iron sanitary sewer utility cover set into square paving tiles. The central field is framed within a prominent ship's steering wheel motif, featuring handles along the outer ring.
