The relief artwork inside the wheel depicts the iconic Yokohama Bay Bridge spanning open water under a sky with stylized clouds and soaring seagulls, with 'YOKOHAMA' prominently cast in English across the top left. The bottom margin includes the Japanese text 'おすい' (Sanitary Sewer) along with technical identification numbers.
notes: A classic Yokohama design that seamlessly combines the city's maritime identity with one of its most recognizable modern engineering landmarks, the Yokohama Bay Bridge.

short_description: Paved Yokohama cover with blue Port of Yokohama emblem.
description: A circular paved-in utility cover set flush within beige cobblestone paving. The surface consists of four textured, stone-like quadrant tiles bounded by a round cast-iron frame. At the center is a small, vibrant blue circular enamel emblem reading 'PORT OF YOKOHAMA 1859' along with a silhouetted lighthouse, harbor facade, and ship. A rectangular cast plate positioned below the center logo reads 'あめ' (Rain / Stormwater).
notes: Designed to blend harmoniously into historic and pedestrian-friendly waterfront walkways, this paved-in variant incorporates the official Port of Yokohama logo, marking the opening of the port in 1859.

short_description: Yokohama Port cover displaying Yokohama Custom House and ship.
description: An unpainted circular cast-iron utility cover set flush into light-colored granite pavers. The central medallion displays a detailed relief scene of a large ocean liner docked in Yokohama Port, framed by the landmark Yokohama Custom House tower (Queen's Tower) under a sky filled with clouds and seagulls. Arching over the top of the scene is the English text 'PORT OF YOKOHAMA', with the historic opening year '1859' cast at the bottom. The top outer rim displays '港湾局' (Port and Harbor Bureau) while the bottom right rim reads '雨' (Rain / Stormwater).
notes: Deployed extensively throughout Yokohama's historic port district, celebrating the city's rich history as one of Japan's primary international gateway ports since 1859.

short_description: Bright yellow Yokohama fire hydrant utility cover with waves.
description: A circular cast-iron fire hydrant access cover set in a concrete collar surrounded by grey street pavers. The outer border and central text box feature high-visibility yellow textured resin fill. The central panel reads '消火栓' (Fire Hydrant) above a serial/pipe diameter plate reading '200'. The background texture above and below the center panel consists of traditional Japanese wave patterns (seigaiha). Near the top center sits the diamond-shaped city crest of Yokohama, and the lower yellow rim is stamped with 'FIRE HYDRANT' in English.
notes: A high-visibility safety cover standard across Yokohama, utilizing bright yellow accents to ensure quick location by emergency fire crews.

short_description: Poké Lid showing Pikachu, Victini, and Munchlax under fireworks.
description: A colorful circular Pokéfuta installed flush in light gray paver stones. The outer ring is light blue with the Japanese characters 'ピカチュウ' (Pikachu), 'ビクティニ' (Victini), and 'ゴンベ' (Munchlax) along the top and right side, with 'YOKOHAMA' and '©Pokémon' along the bottom. The interior scene features Victini floating happily above Pikachu and Munchlax near the Yokohama harbor waterfront, with fireworks exploding above the iconic Yokohama Bay Bridge in the background.
notes: Located along the Sakuragicho / Minato Mirai waterfront area, capturing Yokohama's famous evening skyline and festive fireworks atmosphere.

short_description: Poké Lid featuring Pikachu and Raichu at Red Brick Warehouse.
description: A vibrant circular Pokéfuta set in a cobblestone sidewalk. The outer border contains 'ピカチュウ' (Pikachu) and 'ライチュウ' (Raichu) in katakana, along with '©Pokémon' on the left and 'YOKOHAMA' at the bottom. The inner artwork shows an energetic Pikachu and Raichu leaping forward with yellow lightning bolts, set against the distinctive facade of Yokohama's historic Red Brick Warehouse (Akarenga Soko).
notes: Located near the historic Yokohama Red Brick Warehouse in the Minato Mirai district.

short_description: Poké Lid featuring Psyduck, Pikachu, and Machop near sailing ship.
description: A full-color circular Pokéfuta set flush into granite paving blocks. The light blue outer margin displays the Pokémon names 'ワンリキー' (Machop), 'ピカチュウ' (Pikachu), and 'コダック' (Psyduck), with 'YOKOHAMA' and '©Pokémon' on the sides and bottom. The foreground shows Psyduck peering up alongside Pikachu and Machop, set against a magnificent tall sailing ship gliding through harbor waters.
notes: Situated near Nippon Maru Memorial Park in Yokohama, honoring the famous four-masted sailing ship permanently berthed there.

short_description: Unpainted Yokohama utility cover featuring historic tall sailing ship.
description: An unpainted circular cast-iron utility access cover installed in concrete paver flooring. The inner circular field features a relief illustration of a classic tall sailing ship cutting through ocean waves against a diamond-grid background pattern. The outer rim includes the text 'YOKOHAMA', the waterworks logo, and technical labelling for a soft-seal valve ('ソフトシール弁').
notes: A standard city water department valve cover incorporating Yokohama's signature tall ship imagery.

short_description: Blue sidewalk marker for Kaiko Promenade featuring a sailing ship.
description: A small circular painted sidewalk plaque inlaid directly into textured stone pavement tiles. The plaque features a royal blue outer ring with white text reading '開港の道' (Port Opening Path) at the top and 'KAIKŌ PROMENADE' at the bottom. The center circle depicts a stylized white and green sailing vessel navigating dark water.
notes: A pedestrian wayfinding plaque marking the Kaiko Promenade, a scenic historic walking route connecting key Yokohama waterfront landmarks.
